harvestedTerms.summary: A Hugging Face tutorial walking through deploying a fully local speech-to-speech pipeline on a Reachy Mini robot — covering VAD, STT, LLM serving (llama.cpp/vLLM), and TTS components. The article is primarily technical/instructional and introduces several pipeline-specific implementation patterns.

article.title: LWiAI Podcast #245 - TML-Interaction, Claude For Legal, Sam Altman on Stand

harvestedTerms.summary: Podcast roundup covering OpenAI's new voice API features, Thinking Machines' low-latency conversational model, Claude for Legal, AWS-Anthropic partnership expansion, safety research on CoT grading in RL, and METR's evaluation of Claude Mythos. Primarily a news digest episode touching on multiple fronts.

article.title: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.

harvestedTerms.summary: Google blog post about the Futures Lab partnership with University of Waterloo, where students build AI-powered learning prototypes over eight-week workshops. Highlights include Kanji Garden (AI-generated language stories), SignFluent (real-time ASL feedback), and MuscleMemory (AI camera-assisted exercise coaching). Light on terminology, heavy on product showcase.

article.title: The solution might be cancelling my AI subscription

harvestedTerms.summary: A link post by Simon Willison pointing to David Wilson's reflection on AI-assisted coding leading to runaway project proliferation. The piece explores the attention-fragmenting side effects of low-friction AI tooling — particularly coding agents — and the divergent experiences of neurotypical vs. ADHD users. No major new technical concepts; the value is a human-pattern observation about AI tool use and discipline.

article.title: I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking

harvestedTerms.summary: The Verge investigates Gudtrip, a cannabis vape device marketed as 'the first agentic cannabis device' combining AI, blockchain rewards, and cannabis. The article is primarily a journalist's investigation into a dubious product's claims, exposing the gap between marketing buzzwords and reality. Limited substantive AI terminology is present; the main interest is the misuse of 'agentic' as marketing language.
